constraints_1_content: Before writing /tmp/solution, use execute_bash to enumerate /app/data with `find /app/data -type f` to understand the actual file tree, including hidden files and nested directories, so the script logic can be validated against real data.
constraints_1_rationale: This is specific to the /app/data scan task and references the execute_bash framework tool.
constraints_3_content: Ensure /app/output exists before the script writes the CSV; add `mkdir -p /app/output` as the first side-effect line inside /tmp/solution rather than assuming the directory is pre-created.
constraints_3_rationale: Specific to this task's output path requirement; no framework tool is referenced.
constraints_4_content: To guarantee deterministic row ordering, sort the CSV body with `sort` (lexicographic ascending on the absolute path column) — do not rely on `find`'s filesystem traversal order, which may vary across runs or OS versions.
constraints_4_rationale: Deterministic output is critical for this scan task; the sort strategy is specific to file-path CSV generation.
constraints_6_content:
Use `printf '%s
' "$hits,$path"` (or equivalent quoting) when constructing CSV lines rather than unquoted `echo`, to prevent word-splitting and glob expansion on file paths that may contain spaces or special characters.
constraints_6_rationale: Safe string handling in bash is specific to the CSV-construction logic of this scanning script.
